{{Quote|I was that kid who drew all the time. In middle school, I discovered comics. And I decided: 'I want to do that.'|John Nadeau|<ref name="Winter Park">{{WebCite|url=https://winterparkmag.com/2020/10/05/sequential-artistry/|text=Sequential Artistry|work=Winter Park Magazine|author=Randy Noles|date=2020-10-05|archivedate=20250911092449}}</ref>}}
'''John Nadeau''' is a comic book artist who did pencils and covers for [[Dark Horse Comics]] from [[1996]] to [[2000]]. He drew interiors and covers for a number of issues of ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron]]'', as well as for miscellaneous other [[Star Wars comic books|''Star Wars'' comics]]. ''[[Boba Fett: Twin Engines of Destruction]]'', which he worked on, was voted "Best Original ''[[Star Wars]]'' Comic" by readers of ''[[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine]]''.<ref name="Winter Park" />

Nadeau landed his first professional gig while still a senior in High School, drawing for [[Wikipedia:AC Comics|Americomics']] ''Best of the West''. He has also worked for [[Marvel Comics|Marvel]] and [[Wikipedia:DC Comics|DC]], and he has a degree in Film Production Technology from the [[Wikipedia:University of Central Florida|University of Central Florida]]. Recently, Nadeau has continued drawing comics while also working as a commercial artist and architectural renderer. After taking painting classes at the [[Wikipedia:Crealdé School of Art|Crealdé School of Art]] in [[Wikipedia:Winter Park, Florida|Winter Park, Florida]], he began doing oil paintings for The Art of [[The Walt Disney Company|Disney]] Galleries in [[2018]].<ref name="Winter Park" />
